title: RV8GR 16-bit address mux
purpose: Select the memory address source for fetch cycles or data-access execute cycles.

chips.role: ABUS bits 0-3, IRL low nibble vs PC low nibble
ABUS bits 4-7, IRL high nibble vs PC high nibble
ABUS bits 8-11, DP low nibble vs PC high byte low nibble
ABUS bits 12-15, DP high nibble vs PC high byte high nibble

ports.name: /ADDR_MODE
ports.description: 74HC157 select input; HIGH selects PC, LOW selects {DP,IRL}.

ports.name: ADDR_REQ
ports.description: SRC OR STR; requests data address during T2.

ports.name: A15
ports.description: Same as ABUS15; ROM /CE uses A15 and RAM /CE uses /A15.

behavior.select: The physical select net is /ADDR_MODE. /ADDR_MODE=1 selects PC; /ADDR_MODE=0 selects {DP,IRL}.
behavior.fetch_address: During T0 and T1, /ADDR_MODE remains HIGH, so ABUS=PC.
behavior.data_address: During T2 with ADDR_REQ=SRC OR STR, /ADDR_MODE goes LOW, so ABUS={DP,IRL}.
behavior.warning: Do not drive address mode from raw T2. Real RV8GR uses ADDR_REQ=SRC OR STR, then /ADDR_MODE=NAND(ADDR_REQ,T2).
behavior.a15_decode: ABUS15 selects the memory half: A15=0 enables ROM, A15=1 enables RAM through the inverted /A15 path.

timing.control_window: ADDR_REQ must be stable before T2 data-access use; T0/T1 fetches ignore SRC/STR because T2=0 keeps /ADDR_MODE HIGH.
timing.deadband_boundary: ABUS is not a shared tri-state bus in this package, but 74HC157 propagation delay and downstream setup/hold settle time are still covered by timing_margins.json before any physical speed claim.

guarantees: T0 and T1 stay on PC even when SRC or STR is set
T2 data access uses ADDR_REQ=SRC OR STR, not raw T2
A15 decode is complementary for ROM and RAM

audience: students ages 10-15, still useful for older learners
